name: Clear-History

options.name: -CommandLine
options.description: Deletes command history from a PowerShell session. The string must be an exact match or use wildcards to match commands in the PowerShell session history displayed by `Get-History`. If you enter more than one string, `Clear-History` deletes commands that match any of the strings. The CommandLine parameter can be used with Count .

options.name: -Count
options.description: Specifies the number of history entries that `Clear-History` deletes. Commands are deleted in order, beginning with the oldest entry in the history.

options.name: -Id
options.description: Specifies the command history Id that `Clear-History` deletes. To display Id numbers, use the `Get-History` cmdlet. The Id numbers are sequential and commands keep their Id number throughout a PowerShell session. The Id parameter can be used with Count and Newest .

options.name: -Newest
options.description: When the Newest parameter is used, `Clear-History` deletes the newest entries in the history. By default, `Clear-History` deletes the oldest entries in the history.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the `Clear-History` cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the `Clear-History` cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Deletes entries from the PowerShell session command history.
